title: cedrelopsis longibracteata
intro: Cedrelopsis longibracteata is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapindaceae, native to the eastern coast of Madagascar. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its leaves, which have been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The leaves of the Cedrelopsis longibracteata plant are rich in flavonoids and phenolic compounds, which are believed to contribute to its health benefits, such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. In herbal preparations, the leaves can be consumed as a tea or infused in oil to create a topical treatment for skin conditions and wounds.
